env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with a running RTVI-CV container reachable at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1` (start it with the DEPLOY flow before running these checks — see `eval/deploy-evals.json`). The container must already have at least the readiness, liveness, startup, metrics, and stream-management endpoints reachable. `docker`, `curl`, and `jq` available on the host. The `/stream/add` test below uses the DeepStream sample mp4 at `/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4`, which is baked into the RTVI-CV (DeepStream-based) image — no host-side staging is required. This eval exercises the API USAGE flow of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the live REST endpoint — it must not invoke `/deploy`, redeploy the container, or call any VSS profile. **Recovery preamble (only if the API is unreachable):** before EACH query below, if `curl -sf http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live` returns non-zero (connection refused / curl exit 7), the agent runs `docker start rtvicv-perception-docker` (or `docker restart rtvicv-perception-docker` if it is currently running but unresponsive) and waits for `/api/v1/ready` to return 200 before issuing the rest of the query's API calls. This handles the case where a prior deploy-evals trial used `fakesink` and the DeepStream pipeline exited at EOF after processing all videos (the skill bakes `[tests] file-loop=1` for fakesink/eglsink so this is rare, but the recovery is cheap and idempotent).

expects.query: Add a stream file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4 with id cam_entrance to rtvi-cv.
checks: The agent calls `POST http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/add` against the live RTVI-CV instance.
The request body contains `camera_url` exactly equal to `file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4` and a `camera_id` of `cam_entrance`.
The API call returns a 2xx response (e.g. `STREAM_ADD_SUCCESS` / status 200), confirming the stream was accepted by the live pipeline.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Is rtvi-cv ready? Check the health.
checks: The agent calls all three readiness endpoints: `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live`, `/api/v1/ready`, and `/api/v1/startup`.
The agent uses GET requests (read-only) and does not require per-call user confirmation.
Each of the three readiness calls returns a 2xx response in the trajectory.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: What is the FPS on all streams? Get rtvi-cv metrics.
checks: The `/metrics` response is captured in the trajectory and contains at least one of {per-stream `fps`, `gpu_util`, `GPU_gb`, `cpu_util`}.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: List all active streams in rtvi-cv.
checks: The `/stream/get-stream-info` response is captured in the trajectory and includes the `stream-list` array (each element carries at least `camera_id` and `camera_url`).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
The agent FIRST calls `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` to discover the currently active streams before issuing any removal.

The agent only calls `POST http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/remove` against a `camera_id` that appeared in the `stream-list` response from the prior GET — it does NOT remove any stream id that was not in the live list.
The `/stream/remove` request body contains both `camera_id` and a `camera_url` taken verbatim from the matching entry in the prior `stream-list` response.
The `/stream/remove` API call returns a 2xx response (e.g. `Stream removed successfully` / status 200).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
